channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
item.description: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.content:encoded: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Trump tries to declare victory in Iran as timeline for war's end continues to shift
item.description: President Trump has continued to shift his timeline for ending the U.S.-Israeli-led war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has continued to shift his timeline for ending the U.S.-Israeli-led war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Sen. Tim Kaine on Trump's timeline to end Iran war and efforts to limit war powers
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia about Trump's inconsistent timeline to end the war with Iran and Democrats' efforts to restrain presidential war powers.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia about Trump's inconsistent timeline to end the war with Iran and Democrats' efforts to restrain presidential war powers.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump's timeline for an end to the Iran war continues to shift, U.S. and Iran block the Strait of Hormuz, trapping the Gulf's oil and gas, Trump's allies defend his remarks about Pope Leo.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's timeline for an end to the Iran war continues to shift, U.S. and Iran block the Strait of Hormuz, trapping the Gulf's oil and gas, Trump's allies defend his remarks about Pope Leo.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Thundercat talks about his new album, 'Distracted'
item.description: The bass player known as Thundercat is cherished by fans of funk and jazz, and he recently revealed his love of "yacht rock" in a popular documentary. His new album is called "Distracted."
item.content:encoded: The bass player known as Thundercat is cherished by fans of funk and jazz, and he recently revealed his love of "yacht rock" in a popular documentary. His new album is called "Distracted."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 2 Republicans have a plan to end the DHS shutdown, but there are hurdles to clear
item.description: Top Republicans in Congress have a plan to end the Department of Homeland Security shutdown. But to do so, they'll need to circumvent Democrats and limit defections within their own ranks.
item.content:encoded: Top Republicans in Congress have a plan to end the Department of Homeland Security shutdown. But to do so, they'll need to circumvent Democrats and limit defections within their own ranks.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Hungarian Americans say Orbán's defeat could provide roadmap for countering Trump
item.description: Hungarian Americans say Viktor Orbán's defeat in Hungary provides a blueprint for countering President Trump's attempts to tilt the electoral playing field ahead of the midterms.
item.content:encoded: Hungarian Americans say Viktor Orbán's defeat in Hungary provides a blueprint for countering President Trump's attempts to tilt the electoral playing field ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: 'Everybody is just on edge': Farmer shares how Iran war is affecting his livelihood
item.description: The blockade on the Strait of Hormuz has prolonged fertilizer shortages. NPR's A Martinez asks farm owner John Halcomb how the shortage is affecting his farm and his outlook.
item.content:encoded: The blockade on the Strait of Hormuz has prolonged fertilizer shortages. NPR's A Martinez asks farm owner John Halcomb how the shortage is affecting his farm and his outlook.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Typhoon Sinlaku batters Northern Mariana Islands
item.description: A super-typhoon that made landfall in the Northern Mariana Islands with 150 mph winds shredded trees and roofs, leaving the U.S. territory largely without power.
item.content:encoded: A super-typhoon that made landfall in the Northern Mariana Islands with 150 mph winds shredded trees and roofs, leaving the U.S. territory largely without power.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: U.S. and Iran block Strait of Hormuz, trapping the Gulf's oil and gas
item.description: U.S. Central Command says over 100 aircraft and a dozen warships are enforcing a cordon on Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as Tehran also restricts passage, stranding the Gulf's oil and gas.
item.content:encoded: U.S. Central Command says over 100 aircraft and a dozen warships are enforcing a cordon on Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as Tehran also restricts passage, stranding the Gulf's oil and gas.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy
